connectorUiConfig.title: TheHive (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The TheHive data connector provides the capability to ingest [TheHive](https://thehive-project.org/) security incident response platform data into Microsoft Sentinel through the REST API. Refer to [API documentation](https://docs.thehive-project.org/thehive/legacy/thehive3/api/api-guide/) for more information. The connector provides the ability to get cases, tasks, and alerts from TheHive and visualize them in Azure Sentinel.

dataTypes.name: TheHiveData
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
TheHiveData
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: TheHive API access
customs.description: **TheHive API Version 4 and above** access is required for the TheHive API.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Configuration
instructionSteps.description: Follow the instructions to configure the TheHive connector.

parameters.content: > Get the api Key from your TheHive user profile settings. (or a dedicated user created for this purpose)

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isempty(_createdAt), iff(isempty(_updatedAt), now(), datetime_add('millisecond', tolong(_updatedAt), datetime(1970-01-01))), datetime_add('millisecond', tolong(_createdAt), datetime(1970-01-01))) , Id = ['_id'] , ObjectType = ['_type'] , CreatedBy = ['_createdBy'] , UpdatedBy = ['_updatedBy'] , CreatedAt = ['_createdAt'] , UpdatedAt = ['_updatedAt'] , ObjectTitle = ['title'] , Description = ['description'] , Status = ['status'] , Flag = ['flag'] , TrafficLightProtocol = ['tlp'] , TrafficLightProtocolLabel = ['tlpLabel'] , PermissibleActionsProtocol = ['pap'] , PermissibleActionsProtocolLabel = ['papLabel'] , Number = ['number'] , Severity = ['severity'] , SeverityLabel = ['severityLabel'] , StartDate = ['startDate'] , Stage = ['stage'] , Assignee = ['assignee'] , Tags = ['tags'] , CustomFields = ['customFields'] , UserPermissions = ['userPermissions'] , NewDate = ['newDate'] , TimeToDetect = ['timeToDetect'] , Access = ['access'] , TaskGroup = ['group'] , TaskOrder = ['order'] , Mandatory = ['mandatory'] , ExtraData = ['extraData'] , AlertSource = ['source'] , SourceRef = ['sourceRef'] , Follow = ['follow'] , ObservableCount = ['observableCount'] | project TimeGenerated , Id , ObjectType , CreatedBy , UpdatedBy , CreatedAt , UpdatedAt , ObjectTitle , Description , Status , Flag , TrafficLightProtocol , TrafficLightProtocolLabel , PermissibleActionsProtocol , PermissibleActionsProtocolLabel , Number , Severity , SeverityLabel , StartDate , Stage , Assignee , Tags , CustomFields , UserPermissions , NewDate , TimeToDetect , Access , TaskGroup , TaskOrder , Mandatory , ExtraData , AlertSource , SourceRef , Follow , ObservableCount

metadata.description: Provide the On-premises data gateway that will be used with The Hive connector. Data gateway should be deployed under the same subscription and resource group as playbook.

metadata.title: The Hive - Create alert
metadata.description:
Once a new Microsoft Sentinel incident is created, this playbook gets triggered and performs the following actions:
 1. Parses alert extended properties. 
 2. Parses alert custom details.
 3. Creates alert in TheHive with description, source, sourceRef, title and type passed.
prerequisites: 1. TheHive API Connector has to be deployed prior to the deployment of this playbook under the same subscription.
2. Data gateway should be deployed under the same subscription and resource group as playbooks.
3. TheHive API credentials are required. Refer to the TheHive API Connector documentation.
postDeployment: Once deployment is complete, authorize each connection..
1.  Click the Microsoft Sentinel connection resource

1. In Microsoft Sentinel, analytical rules should be configured to trigger an alert. An alert should contain source and sourceRef custom entities. [Docomentation about custom entities values](https://docs.thehive-project.org/thehive/legacy/thehive3/api/alert/)
2.  Configure the automation rules to trigger the playbook.

metadata.description: Provide the On-premises data gateway that will be used with The Hive connector. Data gateway should be deployed under the same subscription and resource group as playbook.

metadata.title: The Hive - Create case
metadata.description:
Once a new Microsoft Sentinel incident is created, this playbook gets triggered and performs the following actions:
 1. Creates case in TheHive instance with enriched description and title.
2. Gets Hosts, IPs entities.
3. Creates task and bind it to case.
4. Creates observables with hosts and IPs for created case.
prerequisites: 1. TheHive API Connector has to be deployed prior to the deployment of this playbook under the same subscription.
2. Data gateway should be deployed under the same subscription and resource group as playbooks.
3. TheHive API credentials are required. Refer to the TheHive API Connector documentation.
postDeployment: Once deployment is complete, authorize each connection..
1.  Click the Microsoft Sentinel connection resource

1. In Microsoft Sentinel, analytical rules should be configured to trigger an alert. An alert should contain source and sourceRef custom entities. [Docomentation about custom entities values](https://docs.thehive-project.org/thehive/legacy/thehive3/api/alert/)
2.  Configure the automation rules to trigger the playbook.

metadata.title: The Hive - Lock user
metadata.description:
Once a new Microsoft Sentinel incident is created, this playbook gets triggered and performs the following actions:
 1. Parses alerts custom details 
 2. Locks Users by UserId or UserLogin passed from alert.
prerequisites: 1. TheHive API Connector has to be deployed prior to the deployment of this playbook under the same subscription.
2. Data gateway should be deployed under the same subscription and resource group as playbooks.
3. TheHive API credentials are required. Refer to the TheHive API Connector documentation.
postDeployment: Once deployment is complete, authorize each connection..
1.  Click the Microsoft Sentinel connection resource

1. In Microsoft Sentinel, analytical rules should be configured to trigger an alert. An alert should contain source and sourceRef custom entities. [Docomentation about custom entities values](https://docs.thehive-project.org/thehive/legacy/thehive3/api/alert/)
2.  Configure the automation rules to trigger the playbook.

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

TheHive solution provides the capability to ingest common The Hive events into Microsoft Sentinel through Webhooks. The Hive can notify external system of modification events (case creation, alert update, task assignment) in real time. When a change occurs in The Hive, an HTTPS POST request with event information is sent to a callback data connector URL. Refer to Webhooks documentation for more information.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in Preview state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs:

  1. Azure Monitor HTTP Data Collector API b. Azure Functions

Data Connectors: 1, Parsers: 1, Custom Azure Logic Apps Connectors: 1, Playbooks: 3

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
